draft.groundedParagraph: Our churn analysis draws on 100 exit surveys and PostgreSQL drop-off logs spanning a 2M-token context window. Exit surveys reveal that 70% of week-two churners cite pricing as the primary reason for leaving — a signal that behavioral logs alone cannot surface.
draft.speculativeParagraph: Recommend a pricing model evaluation: introduce a mid-tier plan, test annual discounting at the week-two decision point, and align packaging with the value users expect before onboarding investment.

draft.groundedParagraph: Engagement data from 2,400 active users shows that power users (top 10%) account for 62% of feature interactions, while 45% of new signups never activate the core workflow within their first 14 days.
draft.speculativeParagraph: To close the activation gap, we should add a guided feature tour and gamified onboarding badges to drive discovery of advanced capabilities.

draft.groundedParagraph: Funnel analytics across 1,800 trial signups show a 34% completion rate for the five-step setup wizard, with step three (integration configuration) recording the highest abandonment at 22% of all drop-offs.
draft.speculativeParagraph: To improve completion, we should reduce the wizard from five steps to three by deferring integration setup to a post-activation prompt.
